requiredExam.label: Test confrontation visual fields

source.source_section: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate pituitary/sellar mass effect or cranial nerve involvement. Visual field loss changes urgency of MRI review, ophthalmology, and neurosurgical escalation.
requiredExam.rationale: Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).
requiredExam.technique: Test each eye separately by confrontation, comparing peripheral fields with the examiner's field while the patient fixes on a central target.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When pituitary mass, macroadenoma, headache, or visual symptoms are relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: pituitary/sellar mass effect or cranial nerve involvement
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Visual field loss changes urgency of MRI review, ophthalmology, and neurosurgical escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

requiredExam.label: Inspect for galactorrhea

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Galactorrhea changes prolactin interpretation and pituitary workup priority.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect for spontaneous discharge and, only when clinically appropriate, gently express from the areola toward the nipple while preserving privacy and consent.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When prolactin excess, amenorrhea, infertility, breast symptoms, or hypogonadism is relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Galactorrhea changes prolactin interpretation and pituitary workup priority.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect for galactorrhea is supportive for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.action: Add when diplopia, apoplexy, cranial nerve, cavernous is present; this evaluates pituitary/sellar mass effect, cavernous sinus involvement, or cranial nerve III/IV/VI dysfunction. Ophthalmoplegia changes emergency evaluation and neurosurgical urgency.
conditionalExam.rationale: Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).
conditionalExam.technique: Ask the patient to follow a target through cardinal gaze positions and observe for limitation, pain, diplopia, or nystagmus.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When diplopia, apoplexy, cavernous sinus involvement, or cranial nerve symptoms are present

source.source_section: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) curated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

dispositionRules.limitations: This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).

reference_values: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.
Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.
IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
